title: Slow Simmered American White Bean Casserole
description: A hearty and comforting dish featuring dried white beans in a rich and savory braise, perfect for a cold winter's night. This recipe is FODMAP-friendly and made with simple, wholesome ingredients.

ingredients.name: dried white beans
ingredients.notes: such as navy or cannellini beans

instructions: Rinse the dried white beans and soak them in water for at least 8 hours or overnight.
Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the garlic-infused oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion, carrots, and celery, and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 10 minutes.
Add the diced tomatoes, vegetable broth, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Drain and rinse the soaked white beans, and add them to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the casserole for 2 1/2 hours, or until the beans are tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Remove the pot from the oven and stir in the chopped fresh parsley.
Serve the casserole hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
